challenges.title: Labeled Form
challenges.description: Create a form with at least 3 input fields. Every input must have an associated label element using the for attribute that matches the input's id. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a form with labeled inputs -->
<!-- Each label should use the for attribute matching the input's id -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<form' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <form> element'
assert html.lower().count('<label') >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 label elements'
assert html.lower().count('for=') >= 3, 'Each label should have a for attribute'
assert html.lower().count('<input') >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 input elements'
assert html.lower().count('id=') >= 3, 'Each input should have an id attribute'
hints: The for attribute value must exactly match the input's id value
This explicit association lets users click the label to focus the input

challenges.title: Implicit Labels
challenges.description: Create a form with at least 4 inputs. Use explicit labels (for/id) for at least 2 inputs and implicit labels (input nested inside the label element) for at least 2 others. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.starter_code:
html = """
<!-- Create a form mixing explicit and implicit labels -->
<!-- Explicit: <label for="x">...</label> <input id="x">
<!-- Implicit: <label>... <input ...></label> -->

"""
challenges.test_code:
assert '<form' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <form> element'
assert html.lower().count('<label') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 label elements'
assert html.lower().count('<input') >= 4, 'Should contain at least 4 input elements'
assert html.lower().count('for=') >= 2, 'Should have at least 2 explicit labels with for attribute'
import re
nested_pattern = re.findall(r'<label[^>]*>(?:(?!</label>).)*<input[^>]*>(?:(?!</label>).)*</label>', html.lower(), re.DOTALL)
assert len(nested_pattern) >= 2, 'Should have at least 2 implicit labels (input nested inside label)'
hints: Implicit labels don't need for/id because the input is inside the label
